description: Evaluate the transit-oriented development baseline for a proposed East-West Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) corridor in San Francisco using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/brt_catchment_analysis.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Perform a spatial buffer analysis where the corridor is defined as the line segment between the westernmost parcel (minimum x, tie-break with minimum y) and the easternmost parcel (maximum x) in the cleaned dataset (excluding missing or zero coordinates). The catchment area is defined by a buffer distance equal to 5% of the corridor's x-axis width. Calculate citywide and catchment metrics for parcels, residential units, households, population, and jobs. Export the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/catchment_metrics.json using the required schema: {"corridor": {"p_west": {"x", "y"}, "p_east": {"x", "y"}}, "buffer_distance", "metrics": {"parcels", "res_units", "households", "population", "jobs" (each with "citywide", "catchment", "share_pct")}} . Save a scatter plot visualization of the parcels, catchment, and corridor line to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/catchment_map.png. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
